commit.message:
Refactor JSON patching and add runc exec support (#18)

* fix: disable AWS iptables PostStartHook in cilium install

The cilium-agent DaemonSet includes a PostStartHook that runs an
AWS-specific iptables cleanup script. In nested container environments
(DinD/cgroup v1), this hook fails immediately with exit code 2,
causing CrashLoopBackOff before the agent can even start.

Setting cni.iptablesRemoveAWSRules=false disables this unnecessary
hook since we're not running on AWS.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ChQPSjmn1FW8JBVjgxwwgg

* fix: extend runc wrapper to strip caps from exec process specs

The runc wrapper only patched config.json for `runc create`, but
`runc exec` also passes a process spec (via --process flag) that
contains capability arrays. When kubelet or cilium CLI execs into
containers, runc fails with "unable to apply caps: operation not
permitted" because the exec process spec requests CAP_SYS_RESOURCE
which isn't in the bounding set.

Refactor patch_config() to also intercept `runc exec --process <file>`
and strip unavailable capabilities from the process spec JSON.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01ChQPSjmn1FW8JBVjgxwwgg

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Redesign: Gond-editorial aesthetic + BotanistAI cyanotype card

- Full website redesign: editorial cream palette, Cormorant Garamond/DM Sans
  typography, dark Gond-illustrated project cards, compass logo mark
- BotanistAI card: cyanotype + Gond hybrid artwork (deep prussian bg,
  white/sky-blue botanical forms with dot-fill patterns, saffron flower accent)

- Update botanistai.md blog post with corrected tech stack and writing edits

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: always recalculate deduction amounts from source fields

Previously the rent deduction display and calculation used the stored
karensOwes value from Notion, which could be stale (e.g. 1/3 if the
expense was originally Shared (all 3) and the split was later changed).

Now effectiveDeductionAmount() always recomputes from amount+split+paidBy
+category via calculateKarenOwes, so stale stored values are ignored
everywhere: RecurringBanner display, rent API payload, balance Next Rent
preview, and calculateAdjustedRent.

Also fixes the filter in getApprovedDeductions to use the recalculated
value when deciding if an item qualifies.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
v0.1.18 — real test execution, human review gate, code review phase

- verify-run: runs actual test command via child_process.spawnSync, maps
  real output to TCs; blocks self-reporting integrity gap
- verify-complete: requires notes on all fail/skip results
- approve: isTTY-gated human review checklist before any phase approval
- phase review (optional): independent Code Reviewer persona produces
  04_CODE_REVIEW.md with PASS / CONDITIONAL_PASS / FAIL verdict
- reviewer persona: ROLE + CONSTRAINTS + REASONING for skeptical review
- extractTestIndex: includes given/when/then so Developer LLM implements
  to exact TC contracts, not just titles
- phase4 buildBriefing: debug mode auto-injects failing TCs when
  04_TEST_RESULTS.json has fail entries; renamed section to Test Specs
- run-phase: auto-injects failingTests into phase 4 briefing
- 196 tests passing (was 103)

commit.message:
fix: use slot estimation to fetch launch transactions for active tokens

fetch_pool_transactions was returning only the newest 100 transactions
from Helius (newest-first API). For active tokens with 200K+ txns,
this never reached the launch window, making bundle detection and
first-txn analysis blind.

New approach: estimate the Solana slot at pool creation using
current_slot - (time_delta * 2.5 slots/sec), find a block signature
near that slot, then use it as a pagination cursor to jump directly
to the launch window. Reduces API calls from 300+ pages to 3-5.

Tested on DTWgi6rxhB8vLLvduTjDxEE5L3UNy6rtePoV4tQgpump — now correctly
detects 265 bundle wallets capturing 67.5% supply (risk floor 10/10).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Exception Queue tab with ACH debit decisioning and 8 exception categories

Adds a fourth Exceptions tab to the FinOps dashboard with a human-in-the-loop
exception resolution queue. The primary feature is ACH Debit Decisioning where
operators can pay, return (with R01-R29 codes), offset from other accounts,
hold, or escalate inbound ACH debits before the NACHA return deadline. Includes
expandable detail drawers, bulk actions, priority/status filtering, and deadline
countdown. Also populates queue tables for VAN exceptions, reconciliation,
ACH processing errors, ACH returns, RTP/FedNow, payout exceptions, and
infrastructure alerts.

commit.message:
Fix scripts for keycdn (#3)

* Fix duplicate edit fields for sibling instances at different nesting levels

Two fixes for EditFieldProvider resolution:

1. Broaden sibling detection in codegen to search the entire master body
   tree instead of just direct siblings. Instances of the same master
   wrapped in separate container divs (e.g., select dropdowns each in
   their own border div) were not detected as siblings, causing their
   renames to use `defaults` instead of `fields`. Parent defaults then
   intercepted resolution, mapping all instances to the same store key.

2. Add `skipDefaults` parameter to getFieldName() so the key fallback
   in step 1 (component set boundary chaining) skips parent defaults.
   Prevents stale default mappings from intercepting sibling-specific
   field renames.

Also refactors needsWrapperDiv to use node.model.type === 'COMPONENT_SET'
instead of string-matching component names.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add EditableFile codegen emission and redesign component for hover+modal UX

Two changes:

1. CodeGen.ts: Emit EditableFile wrapper for anchor elements with
   contentType 'file', following the same pattern as EditableLink for
   'link'. Wraps the <a> tag as children so the styled download button
   renders normally in non-edit mode.

2. EditableFile.tsx: Redesigned from inline upload UI to hover button +
   modal dialog pattern (matching EditableLink). In edit mode:
   - Shows children (styled download button) with text editable inline
   - Hover reveals "Edit file" icon button
   - Click opens modal with current filename, download link, and upload
   - Neutralizes <a> href via useEffect so contenteditable works inside
     anchors (browsers suppress contenteditable within <a> tags)
   - Filenames decoded from URL encoding for display
   - Dark mode support for modal dialog

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix KeyCDN rsync stale files, pushfin quoting, and cmesh_push_content bugs

- Add --delete flag to rsync in deploy-keycdn.sh and push-keycdn.sh to
  purge stale files (including leftover Drupal files) from the CDN
- Add missing --exclude rules and proper quoting to push-keycdn.sh
- Fix pushfin.sh single-quoted '$bucket' preventing variable expansion
  when calling deploy-keycdn.sh
- Fix invalid CPUQuota=400%% in cmesh-build@.service template
- Fix undefined $command variable in SystemdCommandExecutorService
- Add null-coalescing for $status['command'] in CmeshPushContentForm

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Clerk SignIn silently swallowing errors by using combined flow

The $effect in sign-in/+page.svelte subscribed to $authState, which emits
a new object on every Clerk client state change. A failed sign-in (422)
would trigger unmountSignIn → mountSignIn, destroying the error message
before the user could see it.

Fix: add a mounted guard so the effect runs exactly once, and drop the
separate signUpUrl prop to enable Clerk's combined sign-in/sign-up flow.
Delete the now-unnecessary /sign-up route and update all references.

commit.message:
fix: widen git_sync auto-commit to all tracked files (self-healing)

SCP script deployments modify tracked files outside transport/, blocking
git pull --rebase on the next autonomous sync cycle. Widening from
transport/ + .well-known/ to git add -u prevents this class of failure.

fix(rom-checker): fix scorer word-count bonus causing wrong ROM selection

The same-word-count bonus fired when a candidate happened to have
the same number of words as the target, even with low overlap
(e.g. "Street Fighter Anniversary Collection PS2 USA" vs target
"Street Fighter II Turbo Hyper Fighting"). Replace with a strict
subset check: only bonus when the candidate contains ALL target words.
